channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Landlord recounts events during fatal Porepunkah police shooting
item.description: In August last year, a tragic shooting unfolded in Porepunkah when Dezi Freeman killed two police officers before fleeing into the bush, sparking Victoria’s largest manhunt. For the first time, the Fr...

item.title: Foreign faces hired to boost the image of Chinese businesses
item.description: In China, an unregulated practice exists where foreigners, often called 'white monkeys', are hired to make businesses appear more prestigious or internationally credible. This phenomenon can be traced...

item.title: Reflections on Working at British Vogue in the 1990s
item.description: Charlotte Higgins reflects on her experiences working at British Vogue and The World of Interiors during the 1990s, a period she describes as both glamorous and absurd. She recounts the unique culture...

item.title: Tanzania updates competition laws to ensure fair market and protect consumers
item.description: The Tanzanian government is actively reinforcing its competition laws to improve market efficiency, protect consumers, and attract sustainable investment. Officials say that strong competition policie...

item.title: Urgent Need for Real Policies to Improve Nigerian Workers’ Welfare
item.description: Nigerian workers have shown strong resilience despite plenty hardships like high cost of living, unstable fuel prices, and poor security across the country. This opinion piece highlights how May Day c...

item.title: Cyclists Take a Smoke Break During the 1927 Tour de France
item.description: In 1927, during the grueling Tour de France, a striking photograph captured two cyclists sharing a cigarette mid-race. Unlike today’s elite athletes who follow strict training, diet, and health regime...

item.title: Mahmud Sadis Buba Steps Down From APC House of Reps Race in Sabon Gari
item.description: Mahmud Sadis Buba, also known as Abin Al-Ajabin Zazzau, wey been dey contest for Sabon Gari Federal Constituency seat for House of Representatives don officially withdraw from the race. E post the sta...

item.title: Ena Coach Increases Fares on Nairobi-Upcountry Routes Following Fuel Price Rise
item.description: Kenyans travelling by road should brace themselves for higher travel costs as Ena Coach has raised fares on key routes across the country. The decision comes right after the Energy and Petroleum Regul...

item.title: Hamas military chief Ezzedine Al-Haddad killed in Israeli airstrike in Gaza
item.description: Hamas has confirmed that Ezzedine Al-Haddad, the head of its armed wing, was killed in an Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday. The Israeli military, along with its intelligence agency Shin Bet, claime...

item.title: Conrad Black Calls for Reviving the Commonwealth to Restore Purpose to Canada and the United Kingdom
item.description: In his latest opinion piece, Conrad Black argues that both Canada and the United Kingdom have lost their way due to prolonged mismanagement of foreign policy and domestic affairs. He criticises the UK...

item.title: New compound reduces harmful cholesterol safely in human liver cells
item.description: Researchers have discovered a promising compound that can lower dangerous cholesterol without causing liver damage, a major breakthrough for patients with familial hypercholesterolemia. Traditional tr...

item.title: B.C. boy with autism creates and publishes his own online magazine
item.description: A young boy from British Columbia with autism has launched his own online magazine, showcasing his unique interests and creativity. According to the report, the boy is inspired by a wide range of rand...

item.title: Police deploy 4,000 officers and equipment to manage rival London protests
item.description: On Saturday, 16 May 2026, London witnessed one of the largest police operations in recent years, with the Metropolitan Police deploying around 4,000 officers, armoured vehicles, horses, dogs, drones a...

item.title: Retired Judge and Son Face Dowry Harassment Charges After Daughter-in-Law’s Death in Bhopal
item.description: In a tragic incident in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, a 33-year-old woman, Twisha Sharma, was found dead in her home on May 12, 2026, in what police suspect as suicide. Twisha lived with her husband, lawyer...

item.title: Françoise Dorléac’s Iconic Role as Teresa in Roman Polanski’s Cul-de-sac
item.description: Françoise Dorléac delivered a memorable performance as Teresa in Roman Polanski’s 1966 film Cul-de-sac, portraying a young, bored, and manipulative wife in an isolated castle setting. Unlike typical f...

item.title: Nash Lim Shines as Top Filipino Cyclist in 2026 Tour of Luzon
item.description: In the 2026 Tour of Luzon, foreign cyclists dominated the overall rankings, but 23-year-old Nash Lim emerged as the best-performing Filipino rider. Representing MPT DriveHub, Lim finished fourth in th...

item.title: Uganda records one imported Ebola death after new outbreak in eastern DR Congo
item.description: Health authorities in the region have confirmed a cross-border Ebola incident after the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention verified a new outbreak in the Ituri province of the Democrati...

item.title: Quezon City celebrates the return of La Loma Lechon Festival
item.description: Quezon City is set to bring back the beloved La Loma Lechon Festival, a popular event that highlights the city's rich culinary tradition, particularly its famous roasted pig, or lechon. The festival, ...

item.title: Patrick Cripps reflects on challenging AFL season and expresses loyalty to Carlton
item.description: Carlton captain Patrick Cripps has spoken openly about the tumultuous start to the 2026 AFL season, describing it as ‘career-defining’. The Blues endured a difficult 1-8 start, highlighted by repeated...

item.title: Rohit Pawar Criticises CJI’s Statement on Youth as Intolerant and Hurtful
item.description: Mumbai-based NCP leader Rohit Pawar expressed strong disapproval over Chief Justice of India Surya Kant's recent comment comparing some unemployed youth to cockroaches and parasites. The remark, made ...
